text: Garden Hose pipe is a flexible tube used to convey water. There are a number of common attachments available for the end of the hose, such as sprayers and sprinklers (which are used to concentrate water at one point or spread it over a large area). Garden hose pipe is ideally suited to unlimited applications in a wide range of industries where low pressure work is required for conveyance of water, gardening and other everyday application.

Joking aside, I think having large broms suit the frogs better in that they have larger "pools" to utilise.
Ideally get down to the nearest garden centre for advice and you may see some that could be suitable even though small in size that have broad leaves say and deep areas where water collects.
Smaller Neoregelias, such as Fireball and Super Fireball, would suit smaller vivariums.
Look at any of the neoregelia most stay small. Wild tiger, mini skirt, any of the fireball varieties, domino, alley cat, Tunisia are all nice ones I have tried that don't get very big.

Former Farro Fresh's marketing manager, Ahi McKenzie, has been named as Foodstuffs' new external relations manager. Before Farro, she has been head of marketing and design at The Aromatherapy Company. McKenzie will be working across both Foodstuffs companies, Foodstuffs North Island and Foodstuffs South Island.

Write a page of interior monologue.
Write a page of short sentences, each sentence no longer than seven words.
Write a sentence three hundred words long.

A startling novel powerfully displaying dynamics of sin and redemption, working across time. Something odd is happening to John Faversham, a scientifically minded Englishman of the late 20th century. By chance, he acquires a volume of poems by a minor 19th century poet, who turns out to have lived in his house. Moreover, one of the poems records a vivid dream which has recently been troubling John. How does be come to share a disturbing dream with a long-dead previous owner of his house? As Anthony Duncan's novel unfolds, we discover the roots of the story in the events of the English Reformation, in the 16th century. This sometimes startling tale powerfully displays dynamics of sin and redemption, working across time. But the author avoids any easy moralism. The novel is all the more powerful in its compelling depiction of life's knotted fabric, in which good and evil cannot be easily untangled.

Buzz is beginning to grow about Google's plans for a rolling, significant overhaul of how we experience search. Below are the latest pieces which covered the release of semantic search details last week. It's worth noting how these changes might affect rankings and traffic from the search giant in the future.

Not only is Astrid Chevallier a multi-talented composer, singer and musician, she is also a graphic artist. She designs movie posters. Her artistic talent is evident on her album, both in sound and in the cover art and materials that came with the record.
She is French but sings mostly in English. The music is reminiscent of PJ Harvey's but also of the more ethereal moody style of someone like Kate Bush.
After Cirque is downright creepy. The string intro with its heavy drum and Björkish vocal track gives me chills. Fantastic. This track along with Pluie she does not sing lyrics but rather just vocalizes. She says "there's no language I have heard of yet that could really express what I am going through on songs like Pluie and After Cirque". Indeed. These are beautiful and scary, in a good way of course.
This is an odd but complex and wonderful release.

Wash your tatties and cut into orange segments. Do not peel them as the skin is full of nutritious goodness.
Put them in a bowl and slather them with dark soy sauce. Leave them for a while, stirring occasionally.
Place them on a baking tray, skin down, with shallots and garlic cloves - they need a bit of olive oil.
Roast until they are nicely cooked. They will be brown and crisp with fluffy interiors - a bit like me.

Cut 1 piece from each of the fabrics the width of the blind required plus 2cm, and the drop required plus 6cm. Lay one panel flat, wrong side up, and turn in 0.5cm on all four sides. Iron down and baste. Repeat for other panel.
Put the checked and floral fabric pieces together, wrong sides facing. Pin and baste around both sides and the bottom. Machine-stitch together on three sides.
Insert wooden dowelling at the bottom of the blind and machine-stitch across the blind above it to hold it in place.

Staple the blind and webbing to the batten.
to the back of the batten. This will hold them in place until the batten has been screwed to the wall, and will make the blind and cord easier to handle.
Alternatively, binding the blind with a contrasting fabric is a smart alternative to lining it.

A sensual presentation designed by the author, with full-color illustrations, French flaps, colored endpapers, and high-quality paper.
